'大阪府八尾市東弓削', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yuge-jinja (Yao, Osaka)}} [[JA:弓削神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11487076}} == Japanese content == {{Double image stack|right|Yuge-jinja (Higashi-Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|Yuge-jinja (Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|250|{{Center|Top: Yuge Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Higashiyuge)
Bottom: Yuge Shrine (Yuge-cho, Yao City)}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=36|lat_sec=6.66|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=1.30|label=Yuge Shrine|position=top|width=220|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption={{center|Yuge Shrine location}}}} {{List of coordinates}} ``Yuge Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}. There are two shrines with the same name, both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Overview === {{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai" There are the following two companies in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} as {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Shikinai-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}) which is described as ``Monthly Aihan Shinname''. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yao City {{ill|lt=Higashiyuge|Q11526909|WD=Q11526909|1=Higashiyuge}}) - Formerly enshrined in Higashiyuge Village, Wakae District. Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Yuge Town, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yuge Town, Yao City) - Enshrined in the former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}} Nishi Yuge Village (Yuge Village). Old village company. [[File:Yugedera-ato, zenkei-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Yugiji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|1=Yuge-dera}} (Higashiyuge, Yao City)}}]]The two shrines are located approximately 300 meters apart across the {{ill|lt=Nagase River (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11653384|WD=Q11653384|lt=Nagase River|1=Nagase River}} {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In the middle {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name book|1=UNKNOWN}}" there is a mention of ``Yugego'' in Wakae District, Kawachi Province, and in the Nara period document ``Shosoin Documents'' August 27, 1986, Tenpyoji 6, ``Zosekizanin Rōgekicho''. shows ``Yuge Sukune Hakumaro'' as a person from Wakae District, Kawachi Province, so the area is said to have been a settlement of the ancient clan {{ill|lt=Yuge clan|Q11487069|WD=Q11487069|1=Yuge clan}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Yuge Shrine is said to have been the guardian deity of the Yuge clan from its name, and the location of {{ill|lt=Yugi-ji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|lt=Yuge-ji Temple|1=Yuge-dera}} (later Yugi-ji Temple), which is said to be the Yuge clan's guardian temple, is also known nearby, and there is also a connection with {{ill|lt=Dokyo|Q1272718|WD=Q1272718|lt=Yuge Dokyo|1=Dōkyō}}. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Emperor Koken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Emperor Shotoku|1=Empress Kōken}} ran {{ill|lt=Yugimiya|Q11577605|WD=Q11577605|1=Yuge Palace}} (Nishinokyo) in Ren. The area was the domain of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}}, the former military clan {{ill|lt=Monobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and the Yuge clan was the Banzo clan that was under its control and was in charge of Yugebe. group="Hara">``Newly Selected Surnames and Names'' Kamibesu Sakyo Yuge Sukunejo. In , you can see the description of ``Yuge Sukune'', a second-class member of the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Ibe line|1=Inbe clan}} clan ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenhiwashi Shoya no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and the Monobe line clan (Ishigami same ancestor). "Yuge" "Japanese Ancient Clan Encyclopedia New Edition" edited by Arikiyo Saeki, Yuzankaku, 2015. . In addition, in the Yao City area, there is also the enshrinement of {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao City)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922|lt=Yahagi Shrine|1=Yahagi Shrine, Yao}} (Minamihonmachi, Yao City), which is the guardian deity of the Yahagi clan, which was also in charge of the Yahagi club. , {{ill|lt=Shibukawa Shrine (Yao City)|Q11561593|WD=Q11561593|lt=Shibukawa Shrine|1=Shibukawa Shrine}} (Uematsu-cho, Yao City) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Atobe Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kamei-cho, Yao City) are also known as Mononobe-related shrines.Yuge Shrine is located in ancient Kawachi Province, {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}, Kawachi Province {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}), {{jali nk|Onchi Shrine|Q11492258}} (Yao City, reportedly Ninomiya, Kawachi Province) is said to be the second most prestigious shrine in the world. However, it has since declined and is now dominated by the two companies listed above. The relationship between the two companies' headquarters and branch offices is not clear, and some theories point out that changes may have occurred due to the village moving or branching off due to flood damage from the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but this is not clear from historical materials {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In addition, there is a theory that it is assumed that this is the former enshrinement site, as the small letters ``Kogu'' remain in the land approximately 300 meters east of Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge.The details of the two enshrined deities listed in the "Engishiki" divine name book are also unclear, but "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" . In particular, in "Kawachishi", Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge is designated as the god of life of Yakafuto, and Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town is designated as the god of life of Hikosa Jifutto. Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1986}}, this theory is also found in ``Kawachi Meisho Zue'', ``Shinmeichō Kosho'', ``Dainihonshi Jingishi'', and ``Jingishiryo'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchisha) | 1979}}. In addition, Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town was called ``Futo Daimyojin'' during the Edo period, and a connection has been pointed out {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. Related articles include "{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}" 'Kojiki' Jinmu Tennoki. shows that the sword ({{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|UNKNOWN|lt=Futomitama}}) given by {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} appears to be ``Kafutsujin'' and ``Sashifutsujin'' as alternate names.[{{NDLDC|1913359/172}} "Kafutsujin"] "Great Dictionary of Shinto, Volume 3", Heibonsha, 1941 (link to National Diet Library Digital Collection). . === History === === Outline ===

Founding date is unknown.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=National History|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Cheonan|1=Ten'an}}3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) , it is written that the {{ill|lt=jinka|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Yuge no Kami'' was promoted from the lower rank of junior fifth rank to the higher rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}})There is a theory that it is compared to ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who appear to have progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank. However, since it is a much higher divine rank than Jugoi-jo (Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Rank) in the 3rd year of Tenan, there are strong negative opinions {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}.{{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}"Engishiki" Volume 9 (Shinmyo 1) Wakae District, Kawachi Province. , {{ill|lt=Kawachikuni|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} has "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai As part of the "Monthly Aijo Niiname", two troupes were placed in {{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}, and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{jalin k|Ainamesai|Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is stipulated that it is kept in a heihaku {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. According to a note in the Kongoji book, it is said to have already existed at the ``{{ill|lt=Konin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}'' stage {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In addition, in Kawachi Province, only three shrines were preserved for the Aiga Festival: {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}), {{ill|lt=Onchi Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (Yao City), and Yuge Shrine. According to the Shinmeicho, Yuge Shrines are also known to exist in {{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|WD=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}{{ill|lt=Yatsushiro District|Q1043800|WD=Q1043800|1=Yatsushiro District, Kumamoto}} and {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga Prefecture)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}.{{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|WD=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} In June 1st year ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|WD=Q19676|1=1165}}), in the ``Annual Tribute Order for Shrines and Shrines'', it is listed as ``Yugesha {{sub|Shinbutsu Kosei}}'' along with Hiraoka-sha and Onchi-sha.{{Sfn|Yuge-jinja (Heibon-sha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period are not clear. {{ill|lt=Tenbun (Era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} In the ``Kanshin-ji Annual Revised Kanjo Jinmyocho'' of 20 years ({{ill|lt=1551|Q6560|WD=Q6560|1=1551}}), it appears as ``Yuge Daimyojin'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, one shrine each was enshrined in Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge, as they are today {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, both Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge were established in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinai Shrine) |1979}} is ranked as {{ill|lt=mura shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower junior fifth rank to the upper junior fifth rank ("{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jiroki|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") New Year 27th article of the 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku". - The notation is "Yugegami". {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" July 10th year of the 2nd year of Jogan (860). There is a theory that the ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank, are related to our company {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1 979}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}} (There is also a theory that it is compared to 2 of the 4 dais of Hiraoka Shrine {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine 2 za (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}). === Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City) === {{Shrine |Name = Yuge Shrine |Image = [[File:Yuge-jinja (Higashi-Yuge, Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]

(Higashi-Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|Yuge-jinja (Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|250|{{Center|Top: Yuge Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Higashiyuge)
Bottom: Yuge Shrine (Yuge-cho, Yao City)}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=36|lat_sec=6.66|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=1.30|label=Yuge Shrine|position=top|width=220|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption={{center|Yuge Shrine location}}}} {{List of coordinates}} ``Yuge Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}. There are two shrines with the same name, both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Overview === {{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai" There are the following two companies in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} as {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Shikinai-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}) which is described as ``Monthly Aihan Shinname''. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yao City {{ill|lt=Higashiyuge|Q11526909|WD=Q11526909|1=Higashiyuge}}) - Formerly enshrined in Higashiyuge Village, Wakae District. Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Yuge Town, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yuge Town, Yao City) - Enshrined in the former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}} Nishi Yuge Village (Yuge Village). Old village company. [[File:Yugedera-ato, zenkei-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Yugiji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|1=Yuge-dera}} (Higashiyuge, Yao City)}}]]The two shrines are located approximately 300 meters apart across the {{ill|lt=Nagase River (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11653384|WD=Q11653384|lt=Nagase River|1=Nagase River}} {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In the middle {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name book|1=UNKNOWN}}" there is a mention of ``Yugego'' in Wakae District, Kawachi Province, and in the Nara period document ``Shosoin Documents'' August 27, 1986, Tenpyoji 6, ``Zosekizanin Rōgekicho''. shows ``Yuge Sukune Hakumaro'' as a person from Wakae District, Kawachi Province, so the area is said to have been a settlement of the ancient clan {{ill|lt=Yuge clan|Q11487069|WD=Q11487069|1=Yuge clan}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Yuge Shrine is said to have been the guardian deity of the Yuge clan from its name, and the location of {{ill|lt=Yugi-ji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|lt=Yuge-ji Temple|1=Yuge-dera}} (later Yugi-ji Temple), which is said to be the Yuge clan's guardian temple, is also known nearby, and there is also a connection with {{ill|lt=Dokyo|Q1272718|WD=Q1272718|lt=Yuge Dokyo|1=Dōkyō}}. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Emperor Koken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Emperor Shotoku|1=Empress Kōken}} ran {{ill|lt=Yugimiya|Q11577605|WD=Q11577605|1=Yuge Palace}} (Nishinokyo) in Ren. The area was the domain of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}}, the former military clan {{ill|lt=Monobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and the Yuge clan was the Banzo clan that was under its control and was in charge of Yugebe. group="Hara">``Newly Selected Surnames and Names'' Kamibesu Sakyo Yuge Sukunejo. In , you can see the description of ``Yuge Sukune'', a second-class member of the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Ibe line|1=Inbe clan}} clan ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenhiwashi Shoya no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and the Monobe line clan (Ishigami same ancestor). "Yuge" "Japanese Ancient Clan Encyclopedia New Edition" edited by Arikiyo Saeki, Yuzankaku, 2015. . In addition, in the Yao City area, there is also the enshrinement of {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao City)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922|lt=Yahagi Shrine|1=Yahagi Shrine, Yao}} (Minamihonmachi, Yao City), which is the guardian deity of the Yahagi clan, which was also in charge of the Yahagi club. , {{ill|lt=Shibukawa Shrine (Yao City)|Q11561593|WD=Q11561593|lt=Shibukawa Shrine|1=Shibukawa Shrine}} (Uematsu-cho, Yao City) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Atobe Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kamei-cho, Yao City) are also known as Mononobe-related shrines.Yuge Shrine is located in ancient Kawachi Province, {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}, Kawachi Province {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}), {{jali nk|Onchi Shrine|Q11492258}} (Yao City, reportedly Ninomiya, Kawachi Province) is said to be the second most prestigious shrine in the world. However, it has since declined and is now dominated by the two companies listed above. The relationship between the two companies' headquarters and branch offices is not clear, and some theories point out that changes may have occurred due to the village moving or branching off due to flood damage from the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but this is not clear from historical materials {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In addition, there is a theory that it is assumed that this is the former enshrinement site, as the small letters ``Kogu'' remain in the land approximately 300 meters east of Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge.The details of the two enshrined deities listed in the "Engishiki" divine name book are also unclear, but "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" . In particular, in "Kawachishi", Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge is designated as the god of life of Yakafuto, and Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town is designated as the god of life of Hikosa Jifutto. Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1986}}, this theory is also found in ``Kawachi Meisho Zue'', ``Shinmeichō Kosho'', ``Dainihonshi Jingishi'', and ``Jingishiryo'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchisha) | 1979}}. In addition, Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town was called ``Futo Daimyojin'' during the Edo period, and a connection has been pointed out {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. Related articles include "{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}" 'Kojiki' Jinmu Tennoki. shows that the sword ({{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|UNKNOWN|lt=Futomitama}}) given by {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} appears to be ``Kafutsujin'' and ``Sashifutsujin'' as alternate names.[{{NDLDC|1913359/172}} "Kafutsujin"] "Great Dictionary of Shinto, Volume 3", Heibonsha, 1941 (link to National Diet Library Digital Collection). . === History === === Outline ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center;" |+Kawachi Main God's Divine Floor !Year!!{{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|lt=Hiraoka God|1=Hiraoka Shrine}}!!{{ill|lt=Onchi Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|lt=Onchi God|1=Onji Shrine}}!!Yuge God!!Mikafuto no Mikoto
Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto |- |836||Junior Third Rank, Third Class
→Senior Third Rank, Third Class|| || || |- |839||Senior Third Rank, Third Class
→Junior Second Rank, Third Class|| || || |- |850 years|| ||Senior Third Rank|| |- |856 years||Junior First Rank|| || || |- |859||Junior 1st Rank, 3rd Class
→Senior 1st Rank, 3rd Class||Senior Third Rank, 6th Class
→Junior 2nd Rank, 6th Class||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→Junior Fifth Rank, Upper|| |- |860 years|| || || ||Junior Third Rank
→Junior Second Rank |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Meishin University||Meishin University||Big|| |- |{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya system|1=Ichinomiya}}||Ichinomiya||(denomination) Ninomiya|| || |}
Founding date is unknown.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=National History|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Cheonan|1=Ten'an}}3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) , it is written that the {{ill|lt=jinka|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Yuge no Kami'' was promoted from the lower rank of junior fifth rank to the higher rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}})There is a theory that it is compared to ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who appear to have progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank. However, since it is a much higher divine rank than Jugoi-jo (Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Rank) in the 3rd year of Tenan, there are strong negative opinions {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}.{{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}"Engishiki" Volume 9 (Shinmyo 1) Wakae District, Kawachi Province. , {{ill|lt=Kawachikuni|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} has "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai As part of the "Monthly Aijo Niiname", two troupes were placed in {{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}, and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{jalin k|Ainamesai|Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is stipulated that it is kept in a heihaku {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. According to a note in the Kongoji book, it is said to have already existed at the ``{{ill|lt=Konin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}'' stage {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In addition, in Kawachi Province, only three shrines were preserved for the Aiga Festival: {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}), {{ill|lt=Onchi Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (Yao City), and Yuge Shrine. According to the Shinmeicho, Yuge Shrines are also known to exist in {{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|WD=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}{{ill|lt=Yatsushiro District|Q1043800|WD=Q1043800|1=Yatsushiro District, Kumamoto}} and {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga Prefecture)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}.{{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|WD=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} In June 1st year ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|WD=Q19676|1=1165}}), in the ``Annual Tribute Order for Shrines and Shrines'', it is listed as ``Yugesha {{sub|Shinbutsu Kosei}}'' along with Hiraoka-sha and Onchi-sha.{{Sfn|Yuge-jinja (Heibon-sha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period are not clear. {{ill|lt=Tenbun (Era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} In the ``Kanshin-ji Annual Revised Kanjo Jinmyocho'' of 20 years ({{ill|lt=1551|Q6560|WD=Q6560|1=1551}}), it appears as ``Yuge Daimyojin'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, one shrine each was enshrined in Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge, as they are today {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, both Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge were established in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinai Shrine) |1979}} is ranked as {{ill|lt=mura shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower junior fifth rank to the upper junior fifth rank ("{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jiroki|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") New Year 27th article of the 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku". - The notation is "Yugegami". {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" July 10th year of the 2nd year of Jogan (860). There is a theory that the ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank, are related to our company {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1 979}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}} (There is also a theory that it is compared to 2 of the 4 dais of Hiraoka Shrine {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine 2 za (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}). === Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City) === {{Shrine |Name = Yuge Shrine |Image = [[File:Yuge-jinja (Higashi-Yuge, Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyuge|Q11526909|WD=Q11526909|1=Higashiyuge}}1-166 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees, minutes, seconds|34|36|6.66|N|135|37|1.30|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City)}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Rayaya Nichi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}

The ancestor of the Mononobe clan. * Mikafuto Mikoto * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Shinada Wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Umashi Maji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashi Maji no Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} - Mononobe clan ancestor. * {{ill|lt=Tenhiwashijin|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Tenhiwashi no Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} - ``Shinsen Shojiroku'' ``Shinsen Shojiroku'' Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Yuge Sukunejo. is seen as the ancestor of Yuge Sukune {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. * Hiko Sajihiko no Mikoto * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === History === "Yugejin" in "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Joroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) article (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. {{ill|lt=Edo Period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}2 ({{ill|lt=1831|Q7579|WD=Q7579|1=1831}}) Yahagi Shrine document appears as ``Yuge Shrine''{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. The ancestor of the Mononobe clan. * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Kami Masaru no Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} - Mononobe clan ancestor. * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}} - Enshrined together with Amaterasu Kotai Shrine located in Futamata. === History === "Yugejin" in "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Joroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) article (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. In "Kawachishi" and "Kawachi Kanmeishoki", it appears as "Daimyojin of Yawata Kasuga Gozu Tenno Prefectural Capital" {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) nk|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}, it is ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (shikinaisha)|1979}}. === Precincts === File:Yuge-jinja (Yuge, Yao), 31st|Q2715|WD=Q2715|1=July 31}})→Integrated into Autumn Festival * Autumn festival {{Cite web |title=Shikiyuge Neighborhood Association Federation |url=https://shikiyuge2019.com/ |website=Shikiyuge Neighborhood Association Federation |access-date=2025-07-06 |language=ja}} ({{ill|lt=October 9th|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}}) Saturday and Sunday in late September or the first week of October * New '大阪府八尾市東弓削', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yuge-jinja (Yao, Osaka)}} [[JA:弓削神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11487076}} == Japanese content == {{Double image stack|right|Yuge-jinja (Higashi-Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|Yuge-jinja (Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|250|{{Center|Top: Yuge Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Higashiyuge)
Bottom: Yuge Shrine (Yuge-cho, Yao City)}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=36|lat_sec=6.66|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=1.30|label=Yuge Shrine|position=top|width=220|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption={{center|Yuge Shrine location}}}} {{List of coordinates}} ``Yuge Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}. There are two shrines with the same name, both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Overview === {{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai" There are the following two companies in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} as {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Shikinai-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}) which is described as ``Monthly Aihan Shinname''. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yao City {{ill|lt=Higashiyuge|Q11526909|WD=Q11526909|1=Higashiyuge}}) - Formerly enshrined in Higashiyuge Village, Wakae District. Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Yuge Town, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yuge Town, Yao City) - Enshrined in the former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}} Nishi Yuge Village (Yuge Village). Old village company. [[File:Yugedera-ato, zenkei-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Yugiji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|1=Yuge-dera}} (Higashiyuge, Yao City)}}]]The two shrines are located approximately 300 meters apart across the {{ill|lt=Nagase River (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11653384|WD=Q11653384|lt=Nagase River|1=Nagase River}} {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In the middle {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name book|1=UNKNOWN}}" there is a mention of ``Yugego'' in Wakae District, Kawachi Province, and in the Nara period document ``Shosoin Documents'' August 27, 1986, Tenpyoji 6, ``Zosekizanin Rōgekicho''. shows ``Yuge Sukune Hakumaro'' as a person from Wakae District, Kawachi Province, so the area is said to have been a settlement of the ancient clan {{ill|lt=Yuge clan|Q11487069|WD=Q11487069|1=Yuge clan}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Yuge Shrine is said to have been the guardian deity of the Yuge clan from its name, and the location of {{ill|lt=Yugi-ji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|lt=Yuge-ji Temple|1=Yuge-dera}} (later Yugi-ji Temple), which is said to be the Yuge clan's guardian temple, is also known nearby, and there is also a connection with {{ill|lt=Dokyo|Q1272718|WD=Q1272718|lt=Yuge Dokyo|1=Dōkyō}}. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Emperor Koken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Emperor Shotoku|1=Empress Kōken}} ran {{ill|lt=Yugimiya|Q11577605|WD=Q11577605|1=Yuge Palace}} (Nishinokyo) in Ren. The area was the domain of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}}, the former military clan {{ill|lt=Monobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and the Yuge clan was the Banzo clan that was under its control and was in charge of Yugebe. group="Hara">``Newly Selected Surnames and Names'' Kamibesu Sakyo Yuge Sukunejo. In , you can see the description of ``Yuge Sukune'', a second-class member of the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Ibe line|1=Inbe clan}} clan ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenhiwashi Shoya no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and the Monobe line clan (Ishigami same ancestor). "Yuge" "Japanese Ancient Clan Encyclopedia New Edition" edited by Arikiyo Saeki, Yuzankaku, 2015. . In addition, in the Yao City area, there is also the enshrinement of {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao City)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922|lt=Yahagi Shrine|1=Yahagi Shrine, Yao}} (Minamihonmachi, Yao City), which is the guardian deity of the Yahagi clan, which was also in charge of the Yahagi club. , {{ill|lt=Shibukawa Shrine (Yao City)|Q11561593|WD=Q11561593|lt=Shibukawa Shrine|1=Shibukawa Shrine}} (Uematsu-cho, Yao City) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Atobe Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kamei-cho, Yao City) are also known as Mononobe-related shrines.Yuge Shrine is located in ancient Kawachi Province, {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}, Kawachi Province {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}), {{jali nk|Onchi Shrine|Q11492258}} (Yao City, reportedly Ninomiya, Kawachi Province) is said to be the second most prestigious shrine in the world. However, it has since declined and is now dominated by the two companies listed above. The relationship between the two companies' headquarters and branch offices is not clear, and some theories point out that changes may have occurred due to the village moving or branching off due to flood damage from the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but this is not clear from historical materials {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In addition, there is a theory that it is assumed that this is the former enshrinement site, as the small letters ``Kogu'' remain in the land approximately 300 meters east of Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge.The details of the two enshrined deities listed in the "Engishiki" divine name book are also unclear, but "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" . In particular, in "Kawachishi", Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge is designated as the god of life of Yakafuto, and Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town is designated as the god of life of Hikosa Jifutto. Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1986}}, this theory is also found in ``Kawachi Meisho Zue'', ``Shinmeichō Kosho'', ``Dainihonshi Jingishi'', and ``Jingishiryo'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchisha) | 1979}}. In addition, Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town was called ``Futo Daimyojin'' during the Edo period, and a connection has been pointed out {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. Related articles include "{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}" 'Kojiki' Jinmu Tennoki. shows that the sword ({{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|UNKNOWN|lt=Futomitama}}) given by {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} appears to be ``Kafutsujin'' and ``Sashifutsujin'' as alternate names.[{{NDLDC|1913359/172}} "Kafutsujin"] "Great Dictionary of Shinto, Volume 3", Heibonsha, 1941 (link to National Diet Library Digital Collection). . === History === === Outline ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center;" |+Kawachi Main God's Divine Floor !Year!!{{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|lt=Hiraoka God|1=Hiraoka Shrine}}!!{{ill|lt=Onchi Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|lt=Onchi God|1=Onji Shrine}}!!Yuge God!!Mikafuto no Mikoto

→Junior Second Rank, Third Class|| || || |- |850 years|| ||Senior Third Rank|| |- |856 years||Junior First Rank|| || || |- |859||Junior 1st Rank, 3rd Class

→Junior 2nd Rank, 6th Class||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→Junior Fifth Rank, Upper|| |- |860 years|| || || ||Junior Third Rank
→Junior Second Rank |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Meishin University||Meishin University||Big|| |- |{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya system|1=Ichinomiya}}||Ichinomiya||(denomination) Ninomiya|| || |}
Founding date is unknown.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=National History|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Cheonan|1=Ten'an}}3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) , it is written that the {{ill|lt=jinka|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Yuge no Kami'' was promoted from the lower rank of junior fifth rank to the higher rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}})There is a theory that it is compared to ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who appear to have progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank. However, since it is a much higher divine rank than Jugoi-jo (Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Rank) in the 3rd year of Tenan, there are strong negative opinions {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}.{{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}"Engishiki" Volume 9 (Shinmyo 1) Wakae District, Kawachi Province. , {{ill|lt=Kawachikuni|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} has "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai As part of the "Monthly Aijo Niiname", two troupes were placed in {{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}, and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{jalin k|Ainamesai|Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is stipulated that it is kept in a heihaku {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. According to a note in the Kongoji book, it is said to have already existed at the ``{{ill|lt=Konin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}'' stage {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In addition, in Kawachi Province, only three shrines were preserved for the Aiga Festival: {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}), {{ill|lt=Onchi Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (Yao City), and Yuge Shrine. According to the Shinmeicho, Yuge Shrines are also known to exist in {{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|WD=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}{{ill|lt=Yatsushiro District|Q1043800|WD=Q1043800|1=Yatsushiro District, Kumamoto}} and {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga Prefecture)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}.{{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|WD=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} In June 1st year ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|WD=Q19676|1=1165}}), in the ``Annual Tribute Order for Shrines and Shrines'', it is listed as ``Yugesha {{sub|Shinbutsu Kosei}}'' along with Hiraoka-sha and Onchi-sha.{{Sfn|Yuge-jinja (Heibon-sha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period are not clear. {{ill|lt=Tenbun (Era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} In the ``Kanshin-ji Annual Revised Kanjo Jinmyocho'' of 20 years ({{ill|lt=1551|Q6560|WD=Q6560|1=1551}}), it appears as ``Yuge Daimyojin'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, one shrine each was enshrined in Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge, as they are today {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, both Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge were established in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinai Shrine) |1979}} is ranked as {{ill|lt=mura shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower junior fifth rank to the upper junior fifth rank ("{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jiroki|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") New Year 27th article of the 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku". - The notation is "Yugegami". {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" July 10th year of the 2nd year of Jogan (860). There is a theory that the ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank, are related to our company {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1 979}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}} (There is also a theory that it is compared to 2 of the 4 dais of Hiraoka Shrine {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine 2 za (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}). === Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City) === {{Shrine |Name = Yuge Shrine |Image = [[File:Yuge-jinja (Higashi-Yuge, Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyuge|Q11526909|WD=Q11526909|1=Higashiyuge}}1-166 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees, minutes, seconds|34|36|6.66|N|135|37|1.30|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City)}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Rayaya Nichi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}

(Higashi-Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|Yuge-jinja (Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|250|{{Center|Top: Yuge Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Higashiyuge)

There are two shrines with the same name, both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Overview === {{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai" There are the following two companies in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} as {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Shikinai-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}) which is described as ``Monthly Aihan Shinname''. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yao City {{ill|lt=Higashiyuge|Q11526909|WD=Q11526909|1=Higashiyuge}}) - Formerly enshrined in Higashiyuge Village, Wakae District. Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Yuge Town, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yuge Town, Yao City) - Enshrined in the former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}} Nishi Yuge Village (Yuge Village). Old village company. [[File:Yugedera-ato, zenkei-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Yugiji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|1=Yuge-dera}} (Higashiyuge, Yao City)}}]]The two shrines are located approximately 300 meters apart across the {{ill|lt=Nagase River (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11653384|WD=Q11653384|lt=Nagase River|1=Nagase River}} {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In the middle {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name book|1=UNKNOWN}}" there is a mention of ``Yugego'' in Wakae District, Kawachi Province, and in the Nara period document ``Shosoin Documents'' August 27, 1986, Tenpyoji 6, ``Zosekizanin Rōgekicho''. shows ``Yuge Sukune Hakumaro'' as a person from Wakae District, Kawachi Province, so the area is said to have been a settlement of the ancient clan {{ill|lt=Yuge clan|Q11487069|WD=Q11487069|1=Yuge clan}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Yuge Shrine is said to have been the guardian deity of the Yuge clan from its name, and the location of {{ill|lt=Yugi-ji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|lt=Yuge-ji Temple|1=Yuge-dera}} (later Yugi-ji Temple), which is said to be the Yuge clan's guardian temple, is also known nearby, and there is also a connection with {{ill|lt=Dokyo|Q1272718|WD=Q1272718|lt=Yuge Dokyo|1=Dōkyō}}. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Emperor Koken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Emperor Shotoku|1=Empress Kōken}} ran {{ill|lt=Yugimiya|Q11577605|WD=Q11577605|1=Yuge Palace}} (Nishinokyo) in Ren. The area was the domain of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}}, the former military clan {{ill|lt=Monobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and the Yuge clan was the Banzo clan that was under its control and was in charge of Yugebe. group="Hara">``Newly Selected Surnames and Names'' Kamibesu Sakyo Yuge Sukunejo. In , you can see the description of ``Yuge Sukune'', a second-class member of the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Ibe line|1=Inbe clan}} clan ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenhiwashi Shoya no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and the Monobe line clan (Ishigami same ancestor). "Yuge" "Japanese Ancient Clan Encyclopedia New Edition" edited by Arikiyo Saeki, Yuzankaku, 2015. . In addition, in the Yao City area, there is also the enshrinement of {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao City)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922|lt=Yahagi Shrine|1=Yahagi Shrine, Yao}} (Minamihonmachi, Yao City), which is the guardian deity of the Yahagi clan, which was also in charge of the Yahagi club. , {{ill|lt=Shibukawa Shrine (Yao City)|Q11561593|WD=Q11561593|lt=Shibukawa Shrine|1=Shibukawa Shrine}} (Uematsu-cho, Yao City) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Atobe Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kamei-cho, Yao City) are also known as Mononobe-related shrines.Yuge Shrine is located in ancient Kawachi Province, {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}, Kawachi Province {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}), {{jali nk|Onchi Shrine|Q11492258}} (Yao City, reportedly Ninomiya, Kawachi Province) is said to be the second most prestigious shrine in the world. However, it has since declined and is now dominated by the two companies listed above. The relationship between the two companies' headquarters and branch offices is not clear, and some theories point out that changes may have occurred due to the village moving or branching off due to flood damage from the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but this is not clear from historical materials {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In addition, there is a theory that it is assumed that this is the former enshrinement site, as the small letters ``Kogu'' remain in the land approximately 300 meters east of Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge.The details of the two enshrined deities listed in the "Engishiki" divine name book are also unclear, but "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" . In particular, in "Kawachishi", Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge is designated as the god of life of Yakafuto, and Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town is designated as the god of life of Hikosa Jifutto. Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1986}}, this theory is also found in ``Kawachi Meisho Zue'', ``Shinmeichō Kosho'', ``Dainihonshi Jingishi'', and ``Jingishiryo'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchisha) | 1979}}. In addition, Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town was called ``Futo Daimyojin'' during the Edo period, and a connection has been pointed out {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. Related articles include "{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}" 'Kojiki' Jinmu Tennoki. shows that the sword ({{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|UNKNOWN|lt=Futomitama}}) given by {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} appears to be ``Kafutsujin'' and ``Sashifutsujin'' as alternate names.[{{NDLDC|1913359/172}} "Kafutsujin"] "Great Dictionary of Shinto, Volume 3", Heibonsha, 1941 (link to National Diet Library Digital Collection). . === History === === Outline ===

Founding date is unknown.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=National History|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Cheonan|1=Ten'an}}3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) , it is written that the {{ill|lt=jinka|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Yuge no Kami'' was promoted from the lower rank of junior fifth rank to the higher rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}})There is a theory that it is compared to ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who appear to have progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank. However, since it is a much higher divine rank than Jugoi-jo (Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Rank) in the 3rd year of Tenan, there are strong negative opinions {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}.{{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}"Engishiki" Volume 9 (Shinmyo 1) Wakae District, Kawachi Province. , {{ill|lt=Kawachikuni|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} has "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai As part of the "Monthly Aijo Niiname", two troupes were placed in {{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}, and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{jalin k|Ainamesai|Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is stipulated that it is kept in a heihaku {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. According to a note in the Kongoji book, it is said to have already existed at the ``{{ill|lt=Konin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}'' stage {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In addition, in Kawachi Province, only three shrines were preserved for the Aiga Festival: {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}), {{ill|lt=Onchi Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (Yao City), and Yuge Shrine. According to the Shinmeicho, Yuge Shrines are also known to exist in {{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|WD=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}{{ill|lt=Yatsushiro District|Q1043800|WD=Q1043800|1=Yatsushiro District, Kumamoto}} and {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga Prefecture)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}.{{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|WD=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} In June 1st year ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|WD=Q19676|1=1165}}), in the ``Annual Tribute Order for Shrines and Shrines'', it is listed as ``Yugesha {{sub|Shinbutsu Kosei}}'' along with Hiraoka-sha and Onchi-sha.{{Sfn|Yuge-jinja (Heibon-sha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period are not clear. {{ill|lt=Tenbun (Era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} In the ``Kanshin-ji Annual Revised Kanjo Jinmyocho'' of 20 years ({{ill|lt=1551|Q6560|WD=Q6560|1=1551}}), it appears as ``Yuge Daimyojin'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, one shrine each was enshrined in Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge, as they are today {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, both Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge were established in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinai Shrine) |1979}} is ranked as {{ill|lt=mura shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower junior fifth rank to the upper junior fifth rank ("{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jiroki|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") New Year 27th article of the 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku". - The notation is "Yugegami". {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" July 10th year of the 2nd year of Jogan (860). There is a theory that the ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank, are related to our company {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1 979}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}} (There is also a theory that it is compared to 2 of the 4 dais of Hiraoka Shrine {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine 2 za (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}). === Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City) === {{Shrine |Name = Yuge Shrine |Image = [[File:Yuge-jinja (Higashi-Yuge, Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]

'大阪府八尾市東弓削', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yuge-jinja (Yao, Osaka)}} [[JA:弓削神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11487076|commons|Category:Yuge-jinja (Yao, Osaka)|ja|弓削神社 (八尾市)}} == Japanese content == {{Double image stack|right|Yuge-jinja (Higashi-Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|Yuge-jinja (Yuge, Yao), haiden.jpg|250|{{Center|Top: Yuge Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Higashiyuge)
Bottom: Yuge Shrine (Yuge-cho, Yao City)}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=36|lat_sec=6.66|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=1.30|label=Yuge Shrine|position=top|width=220|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption={{center|Yuge Shrine location}}}} {{List of coordinates}} ``Yuge Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}. There are two shrines with the same name, both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Overview === {{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai" There are the following two companies in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} as {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Shikinai-taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}) which is described as ``Monthly Aihan Shinname''. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yao City {{ill|lt=Higashiyuge|Q11526909|WD=Q11526909|1=Higashiyuge}}) - Formerly enshrined in Higashiyuge Village, Wakae District. Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # '''{{ill|lt=#Yuge Shrine (Yuge Town, Yao City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}}''' (Yuge Town, Yao City) - Enshrined in the former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}} Nishi Yuge Village (Yuge Village). Old village company. [[File:Yugedera-ato, zenkei-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Yugiji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|1=Yuge-dera}} (Higashiyuge, Yao City)}}]]The two shrines are located approximately 300 meters apart across the {{ill|lt=Nagase River (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11653384|WD=Q11653384|lt=Nagase River|1=Nagase River}} {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In the middle {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name book|1=UNKNOWN}}" there is a mention of ``Yugego'' in Wakae District, Kawachi Province, and in the Nara period document ``Shosoin Documents'' August 27, 1986, Tenpyoji 6, ``Zosekizanin Rōgekicho''. shows ``Yuge Sukune Hakumaro'' as a person from Wakae District, Kawachi Province, so the area is said to have been a settlement of the ancient clan {{ill|lt=Yuge clan|Q11487069|WD=Q11487069|1=Yuge clan}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Yuge Shrine is said to have been the guardian deity of the Yuge clan from its name, and the location of {{ill|lt=Yugi-ji Temple Ruins|Q28684331|WD=Q28684331|lt=Yuge-ji Temple|1=Yuge-dera}} (later Yugi-ji Temple), which is said to be the Yuge clan's guardian temple, is also known nearby, and there is also a connection with {{ill|lt=Dokyo|Q1272718|WD=Q1272718|lt=Yuge Dokyo|1=Dōkyō}}. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Emperor Koken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Emperor Shotoku|1=Empress Kōken}} ran {{ill|lt=Yugimiya|Q11577605|WD=Q11577605|1=Yuge Palace}} (Nishinokyo) in Ren. The area was the domain of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}}, the former military clan {{ill|lt=Monobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and the Yuge clan was the Banzo clan that was under its control and was in charge of Yugebe. group="Hara">``Newly Selected Surnames and Names'' Kamibesu Sakyo Yuge Sukunejo. In , you can see the description of ``Yuge Sukune'', a second-class member of the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Ibe line|1=Inbe clan}} clan ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenhiwashi Shoya no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and the Monobe line clan (Ishigami same ancestor). "Yuge" "Japanese Ancient Clan Encyclopedia New Edition" edited by Arikiyo Saeki, Yuzankaku, 2015. . In addition, in the Yao City area, there is also the enshrinement of {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao City)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922|lt=Yahagi Shrine|1=Yahagi Shrine, Yao}} (Minamihonmachi, Yao City), which is the guardian deity of the Yahagi clan, which was also in charge of the Yahagi club. , {{ill|lt=Shibukawa Shrine (Yao City)|Q11561593|WD=Q11561593|lt=Shibukawa Shrine|1=Shibukawa Shrine}} (Uematsu-cho, Yao City) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Atobe Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kamei-cho, Yao City) are also known as Mononobe-related shrines.Yuge Shrine is located in ancient Kawachi Province, {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}, Kawachi Province {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}), {{jali nk|Onchi Shrine|Q11492258}} (Yao City, reportedly Ninomiya, Kawachi Province) is said to be the second most prestigious shrine in the world. However, it has since declined and is now dominated by the two companies listed above. The relationship between the two companies' headquarters and branch offices is not clear, and some theories point out that changes may have occurred due to the village moving or branching off due to flood damage from the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but this is not clear from historical materials {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In addition, there is a theory that it is assumed that this is the former enshrinement site, as the small letters ``Kogu'' remain in the land approximately 300 meters east of Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge.The details of the two enshrined deities listed in the "Engishiki" divine name book are also unclear, but "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" . In particular, in "Kawachishi", Yuge Shrine in Higashi-Yuge is designated as the god of life of Yakafuto, and Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town is designated as the god of life of Hikosa Jifutto. Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1986}}, this theory is also found in ``Kawachi Meisho Zue'', ``Shinmeichō Kosho'', ``Dainihonshi Jingishi'', and ``Jingishiryo'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchisha) | 1979}}. In addition, Yuge Shrine in Yuge Town was called ``Futo Daimyojin'' during the Edo period, and a connection has been pointed out {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. Related articles include "{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}" 'Kojiki' Jinmu Tennoki. shows that the sword ({{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|UNKNOWN|lt=Futomitama}}) given by {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} appears to be ``Kafutsujin'' and ``Sashifutsujin'' as alternate names.[{{NDLDC|1913359/172}} "Kafutsujin"] "Great Dictionary of Shinto, Volume 3", Heibonsha, 1941 (link to National Diet Library Digital Collection). . === History === === Outline ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center;" |+Kawachi Main God's Divine Floor !Year!!{{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|lt=Hiraoka God|1=Hiraoka Shrine}}!!{{ill|lt=Onchi Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|lt=Onchi God|1=Onji Shrine}}!!Yuge God!!Mikafuto no Mikoto
Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto |- |836||Junior Third Rank, Third Class
→Senior Third Rank, Third Class|| || || |- |839||Senior Third Rank, Third Class
→Junior Second Rank, Third Class|| || || |- |850 years|| ||Senior Third Rank|| |- |856 years||Junior First Rank|| || || |- |859||Junior 1st Rank, 3rd Class
→Senior 1st Rank, 3rd Class||Senior Third Rank, 6th Class
→Junior 2nd Rank, 6th Class||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→Junior Fifth Rank, Upper|| |- |860 years|| || || ||Junior Third Rank
→Junior Second Rank |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Meishin University||Meishin University||Big|| |- |{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya system|1=Ichinomiya}}||Ichinomiya||(denomination) Ninomiya|| || |}
Founding date is unknown.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=National History|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Cheonan|1=Ten'an}}3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) , it is written that the {{ill|lt=jinka|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Yuge no Kami'' was promoted from the lower rank of junior fifth rank to the higher rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}})There is a theory that it is compared to ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who appear to have progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank. However, since it is a much higher divine rank than Jugoi-jo (Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Rank) in the 3rd year of Tenan, there are strong negative opinions {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}.{{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}"Engishiki" Volume 9 (Shinmyo 1) Wakae District, Kawachi Province. , {{ill|lt=Kawachikuni|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}} has "Yuge Shrine Niza Dai As part of the "Monthly Aijo Niiname", two troupes were placed in {{ill|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinaisha}}, and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{jalin k|Ainamesai|Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is stipulated that it is kept in a heihaku {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. According to a note in the Kongoji book, it is said to have already existed at the ``{{ill|lt=Konin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}'' stage {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In addition, in Kawachi Province, only three shrines were preserved for the Aiga Festival: {{ill|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|Q32422|WD=Q32422|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Higashiosaka City|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}), {{ill|lt=Onchi Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (Yao City), and Yuge Shrine. According to the Shinmeicho, Yuge Shrines are also known to exist in {{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|WD=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}{{ill|lt=Yatsushiro District|Q1043800|WD=Q1043800|1=Yatsushiro District, Kumamoto}} and {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga Prefecture)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}.{{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|WD=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} In June 1st year ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|WD=Q19676|1=1165}}), in the ``Annual Tribute Order for Shrines and Shrines'', it is listed as ``Yugesha {{sub|Shinbutsu Kosei}}'' along with Hiraoka-sha and Onchi-sha.{{Sfn|Yuge-jinja (Heibon-sha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period are not clear. {{ill|lt=Tenbun (Era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} In the ``Kanshin-ji Annual Revised Kanjo Jinmyocho'' of 20 years ({{ill|lt=1551|Q6560|WD=Q6560|1=1551}}), it appears as ``Yuge Daimyojin'' {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, one shrine each was enshrined in Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge, as they are today {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, both Higashi-Yuge and Nishi-Yuge were established in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikinai Shrine) |1979}} is ranked as {{ill|lt=mura shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower junior fifth rank to the upper junior fifth rank ("{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jiroki|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") New Year 27th article of the 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku". - The notation is "Yugegami". {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}}2 years ({{ill|lt=860 years|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" July 10th year of the 2nd year of Jogan (860). There is a theory that the ``Yakafuto no Mikoto'' and ``Hikosa Jifuto no Mikoto'' of Kawachi Province, who progressed from Junior Third Rank to Junior Second Rank, are related to our company {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1 979}}{{Sfn|Yuge Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}} (There is also a theory that it is compared to 2 of the 4 dais of Hiraoka Shrine {{Sfn|Yuge Shrine 2 za (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}). === Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City) === {{Shrine |Name = Yuge Shrine |Image = [[File:Yuge-jinja (Higashi-Yuge, Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyuge|Q11526909|WD=Q11526909|1=Higashiyuge}}1-166 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees, minutes, seconds|34|36|6.66|N|135|37|1.30|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Yuge Shrine (Higashiyuge, Yao City)}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Rayaya Nichi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}
